mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals supply-side contraction and inventory buildup, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL differential shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns (t-stat < 1.5) across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a broad economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during credit tightening phases.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.5% over 3 months.
